concerns.text: FR-006 requires mapping top-loading tokens to semantic categories using 'Universal Dependencies (UD) treebank resources'. The plan.md mentions generating 'top-loading token lists' in Phase 4 and 'report.py', but contains no explicit task, data model entity, or contract definition for the UD lexicon ingestion, token-to-POS mapping logic, or the calculation of the 'semantic proportion' required by SC-005. Without a specific plan element for this external resource integration, the implementer has no task to satisfy FR-006 or measure SC-005.

FR-003 mandates 'verify frequency vector convergence (KL-divergence < 0.01 against a larger sample)'. The plan.md describes downloading frequency lists and checksumming them (Constitution Check, Phase 0), but does not include a specific phase, script, or data model entity for performing the KL-divergence convergence check against a larger sample. The 'research.md' mentions 'frequency vector convergence' as a variable fit check but does not detail the implementation of the KL-divergence calculation or the 'larger sample' reference. This leaves FR-003 and its associated validation unbacked by a concrete plan element.

SC-005 requires measuring the 'proportion of top-loading tokens identified as function words vs. content words' against the source corpus. This measurement depends entirely on the semantic categorization logic in FR-006. Since the plan lacks a specific element for the UD treebank integration and categorization logic (see FR-006 concern), there is no plan element to generate the data required to measure SC-005.
